channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: ACECQA invites services to register interest in Quality Support Program
item.description: Eligible early childhood education and care (ECEC) services are being encouraged to register their interest in participating in the Australian Children's Education and Care Quality Authority's (ACECQA) Quality Support Program (QSP), a tailored initiative designed to help services strengthen quality practice, build leadership capability and support continuous improvement under the National Quality Framework (NQF).
item.content:encoded: Eligible early childhood education and care (ECEC) services are being encouraged to register their interest in participating in the Australian Children's Education and Care Quality Authority's (ACECQA) Quality Support Program (QSP), a tailored initiative designed to help services strengthen quality practice, build leadership capability and support continuous improvement under the National Quality Framework (NQF).
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: New paid pathway opens door to teaching careers in Tasmania
item.description: A new paid employment-based pathway launched by the University of Tasmania is set to make teaching careers more accessible, offering career changers and university graduates the opportunity to earn an income while completing their teaching qualification.
item.content:encoded: A new paid employment-based pathway launched by the University of Tasmania is set to make teaching careers more accessible, offering career changers and university graduates the opportunity to earn an income while completing their teaching qualification.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children's Day 2026 invites Australia to embrace 'Living Our Truth'
item.description: Early childhood education and care (ECEC) services across Australia are being encouraged to begin planning their celebrations for National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children's Day, with SNAICC – National Voice for our Children announcing the 2026 theme, Living Our Truth.
item.content:encoded: Early childhood education and care (ECEC) services across Australia are being encouraged to begin planning their celebrations for National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children's Day, with SNAICC – National Voice for our Children announcing the 2026 theme, Living Our Truth.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: AI is reshaping the future of work but childcare may remain one of the most human professions
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming workplaces around the world, with a recent World Economic Forum (WEF) analysis warning that many entry-level roles are set to change dramatically as automation accelerates. While industries built around routine administrative tasks face significant disruption, early childhood education and care (ECEC) may be better positioned than many others because it relies on skills that technology cannot easily replicate.
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming workplaces around the world, with a recent World Economic Forum (WEF) analysis warning that many entry-level roles are set to change dramatically as automation accelerates. While industries built around routine administrative tasks face significant disruption, early childhood education and care (ECEC) may be better positioned than many others because it relies on skills that technology cannot easily replicate.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Beyond visibility: Why effective supervision is about more than keeping children in sight
item.description: Supervision is often discussed in terms of ratios, visibility and compliance. While these remain essential components of safe practice, ACECQA is encouraging educators to rethink supervision as something much broader, a reflective, relationship-based practice built on professional judgement, deep knowledge of children and intentional engagement.
item.content:encoded: Supervision is often discussed in terms of ratios, visibility and compliance. While these remain essential components of safe practice, ACECQA is encouraging educators to rethink supervision as something much broader, a reflective, relationship-based practice built on professional judgement, deep knowledge of children and intentional engagement.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Guardian Childcare teams receive 4.75% pay increase - newsreel.com.au
item.description: Guardian Childcare teams receive 4.75% pay increase - newsreel.com.au
item.content:encoded: Guardian Childcare teams receive 4.75% pay increase - newsreel.com.au
item.dc:creator: newsreel.com.au

item.title: Renewal of the wage subsidy great news for educators, children, families and gender equity
item.description: The renewal of the Australian Government’s 15% early learning wage subsidy marks a significant step forward for the sector, recognising the essential role educators play in supporting children’s learning and development.
item.content:encoded: The renewal of the Australian Government’s 15% early learning wage subsidy marks a significant step forward for the sector, recognising the essential role educators play in supporting children’s learning and development.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: SNAICC says Aboriginal-led ECEC must be central to Thriving Kids reform
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has welcomed the Australian Government's proposed Thriving Kids initiative as a significant opportunity to improve developmental and disability support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children, while warning the reform will only succeed if Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations are genuine partners in its design and delivery.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has welcomed the Australian Government's proposed Thriving Kids initiative as a significant opportunity to improve developmental and disability support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children, while warning the reform will only succeed if Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations are genuine partners in its design and delivery.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 26 June 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, national attention turns to educator workforce investment, continued coverage of child protection reforms in Queensland and the NT, and Children’s Day.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, national attention turns to educator workforce investment, continued coverage of child protection reforms in Queensland and the NT, and Children’s Day.

item.title: Thriving Kids must be built with Aboriginal communities to succeed
item.description: SNAICC - National Voice for our Children has released a new policy paper calling on governments to design and deliver the Thriving Kids initiative in genuine partnership with Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations (ACCOs).
item.content:encoded: SNAICC - National Voice for our Children has released a new policy paper calling on governments to design and deliver the Thriving Kids initiative in genuine partnership with Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations (ACCOs).
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Investing in educators means investing in children
item.description: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
